plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall market exposure. Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk, aligning with capital preservation. Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% for stable income and broad market exposure. Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single-name concentration.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets approximately 1.5% per month by prioritizing capital preservation and stability. Risk is significantly reduced by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration, but potential upside is limited. Expect lower volatility and drawdowns, with a focus on consistent, albeit smaller, gains.

moves.note: Increase exposure to strong tech name
New quality tech add, good R/R

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall leverage. Reduce SOXL and SPAL to 3% and 2% respectively, keeping minimal leveraged exposure. Increase QQQI to 20% to maintain a balanced income component. Add to CRWD, bringing it to 15%, capitalizing on its strong scanner read.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets approximately 2.5% per month by blending risk-first growth with controlled exposure. It reduces leverage and concentration while still participating in quality tech upside. Expect moderate volatility and drawdowns, with a focus on sustainable growth.

moves.note: Reduce broad semi exposure, focus SOXL

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and free up capital for higher-conviction plays. Increase SOXL to 8%, leaning into leveraged semiconductor exposure. Add to CRWD, bringing it to 20%, concentrating in this strong AI/Software name. Buy PANW for 10% and NOW for 7% as new high-conviction tech additions.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets approximately 4% per month by stretching growth and using leverage. It significantly increases exposure to high-scoring tech and leveraged ETFs, amplifying both potential upside and downside. Be aware of higher volatility, significant drawdowns, and the daily decay/rebalancing effects of leveraged ETFs, requiring strict stop discipline.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and shift focus to income. Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with income and low drawdown. Increase QQQI to 30% to maximize income generation as a core holding. Trim QQQ to 10% and CRWD to 5% to reduce growth and high-beta single-name exposure.
income.read: This income plan targets approximately 1% per month by prioritizing steady monthly distributions and low drawdown. It significantly reduces leverage and high-beta single names, focusing on income-generating ETFs and broad market exposure. While aiming for reliable income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in market selloffs; this is not a savings account.
